page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. With a single thread per core, the system provides predictable, dedicated compute performance without hyper-threading overhead.

The server is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be handled via network-attached options. To support high-throughput data transfers, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

As a bare-metal instance, the c8gb.metal-48xl offers direct hardware access, which eliminates virtualization overhead. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for large-scale, parallelized workloads that benefit from arm64 architecture optimization. Recommended workloads include high-performance computing, intensive batch processing, distributed analytics, and large-scale web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by AWS Graviton arm64 processors. The instance features 192 dedicated physical cores running at 2.7 GHz with a single thread per core, ensuring predictable performance without hyper-threading overhead. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. The server does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage options. It features a high-capacity network interface with a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Designed for direct hardware access and high resource density, this bare-metal server is optimized for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, and distributed analytics that leverage the arm64 architecture.
